{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YFPrR3LaV2gU7b1pRhi3c3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/*\n     2→ * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3→ * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4→ * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5→ * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6→ * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7→ * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8→ *\n     9→ *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10→ *\n    11→ * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12→ * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13→ * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14→ * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15→ * limitations under the License.\n    16→ */\n    17→package org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n    18→\n    19→import org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\n    20→import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n    21→import org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\n    22→import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\n    23→import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\n    24→import org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\n    25→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\n    26→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\n    27→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\n    28→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n    29→import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n    30→\n    31→import java.io.IOException;\n    32→import java.io.InputStream;\n    33→import java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\n    34→import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\n    35→import java.util.ArrayList;\n    36→import java.util.Collections;\n    37→import java.util.Iterator;\n    38→import java.util.List;\n    39→import java.util.NoSuchElementException;\n    40→import java.util.Objects;\n    41→import java.util.OptionalLong;\n    42→\n    43→import static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n    44→\n    45→/**\n    46→ * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:\n    47→ *\n    48→ * RecordBatch =>\n    49→ *  BaseOffset => Int64\n    50→ *  Length => Int32\n    51→ *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n    52→ *  Magic => Int8\n    53→ *  CRC => Uint32\n    54→ *  Attributes => Int16\n    55→ *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n    56→ *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n    57→ *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n    58→ *  ProducerId => Int64\n    59→ *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n    60→ *  BaseSequence => Int32\n    61→ *  RecordsCount => Int32\n    62→ *  Records => [Record]\n    63→ *\n    64→ * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized\n    65→ * directly following the count of the number of records.\n    66→ *\n    67→ * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is\n    68→ * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret\n    69→ * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC\n    70→ * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by\n    71→ * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.\n    72→ *\n    73→ * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence\n    74→ * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the\n    75→ * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following\n    76→ * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence\n    77→ * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an\n    78→ * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence error, which is typically fatal. The base sequence number must be preserved for\n    79→ * duplicate checking: the broker checks incoming Produce requests for duplicates by verifying that the first and\n    80→ * last sequence numbers of the incoming batch match the last from that producer.\n    81→ *\n    82→ * Note that if all of the records in a batch are removed during compaction, the broker may still retain an empty\n    83→ * batch header in order to preserve the producer sequence information as described above. These empty batches\n    84→ * are retained only until either a new sequence number is written by the corresponding producer or the producerId\n    85→ * is expired from lack of activity.\n    86→ *\n    87→ * There is no similar need to preserve the timestamp from the original batch after compaction. The BaseTimestamp\n    88→ * field therefore reflects the timestamp of the first record in the batch in most cases. If the batch is empty, the\n    89→ * BaseTimestamp will be set to -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP). If the delete horizon flag is set to 1, the BaseTimestamp\n    90→ * will be set to the time at which tombstone records and aborted transaction markers in the batch should be removed.\n    91→ *\n    92→ * Similarly, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the maximum timestamp of the current records if the timestamp type\n    93→ * is CREATE_TIME. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, on the other hand, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the timestamp set\n    94→ * by the broker and is preserved after compaction. Additionally, the MaxTimestamp of an empty batch always retains\n    95→ * the previous value prior to becoming empty.\n    96→ *\n    97→ * The current attributes are given below:\n    98→ *\n    99→ *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   100→ *  | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |\n   101→ *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   102→ */\n   103→public class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n   104→    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n   105→    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n   106→    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n   107→    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n   108→    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n   109→    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n   110→    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n   111→    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n   112→    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n   113→    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n   114→    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n   115→    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n   116→    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n   117→    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n   118→    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n   119→    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n   120→    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n   121→    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n   122→    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n   123→    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n   124→    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n   125→    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n   126→    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n   127→    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n   128→    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n   129→    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n   130→    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n   131→    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n   132→\n   133→    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n   134→    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n   135→    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n   136→    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n   137→    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n   138→\n   139→    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n   140→\n   141→    DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n   142→        this.buffer = buffer;\n   143→    }\n   144→\n   145→    @Override\n   146→    public byte magic() {\n   147→        return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n   148→    }\n   149→\n   150→    @Override\n   151→    public void ensureValid() {\n   152→        if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n   153→            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n   154→                    \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n   155→\n   156→        if (!isValid())\n   157→            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n   158→                    + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n   159→    }\n   160→\n   161→    /**\n   162→     * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.\n   163→     *\n   164→     * @return The base timestamp\n   165→     */\n   166→    public long baseTimestamp() {\n   167→        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n   168→    }\n   169→\n   170→    @Override\n   171→    public long maxTimestamp() {\n   172→        return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n   173→    }\n   174→\n   175→    @Override\n   176→    public TimestampType timestampType() {\n   177→        return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\n   178→    }\n   179→\n   180→    @Override\n   181→    public long baseOffset() {\n   182→        return 189→\n   190→    @Override\n   191→    public long producerId() {\n   192→        return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\n   193→    }\n   194→\n   195→    @Override\n   196→    public short   @Override\n   210→    public int lastSequence() {\n   211→        int baseSequence = baseSequence();\n   212→        if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\n   213→            return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\n   214→        return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\n   215→    }\n   216→\n   217→    @Override\n   218→    public CompressionType compressionType() {\n   219→        return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\n   220→    }\n   221→\n   222→    @Override\n   223→    public int sizeInBytes() {\n   224→        return @Override\n   232→    public Integer countOrNull() {\n   233→        return count();\n   234→    }\n   235→\n   236→    @Override\n   237→    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n   238→        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\n   239→    }\n   240→\n   241→    @Override\n   242→    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\n   243→        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\n   244→    }\n   245→\n   246→    @Override\n   247→    public boolean isTransactional() {\n   248→        return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\n   252→        return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n   253→    }\n   254→\n   255→    @Override\n   256→    public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n   257→        if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\n   258→            return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\n   259→        else\n   260→            return OptionalLong.empty();\n   261→    }\n   262→\n   263→    @Override\n   264→    public boolean isControlBatch() {\n   265→        return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n   266→    }\n   267→\n   268→    @Override\n   269→    public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n   270→        return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n   271→    }\n   272→\n   273→    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n   274→        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n   275→        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n   276→        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\n   277→    }\n   278→\n   279→    private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\n   280→        final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\n   281→\n   282→        if (skipKeyValue) {\n   283→            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n   284→                @Override\n   285→                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n   286→                    return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n   287→                }\n   288→            };\n   289→        } else {\n   290→            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n   291→                @Override\n   292→                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n   293→                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n   294→                }\n   295→            };\n   296→        }\n   297→    }\n   298→\n   299→    private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\n   300→        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n   301→        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n   302→        return new RecordIterator() {\n   303→            @Override\n   304→            protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n   305→                try {\n   306→                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n   307→                } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\n   308→                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\");\n   309→                }\n   310→            }\n   311→            @Override\n   312→            protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n   313→                return !buffer.hasRemaining();\n   314→            }\n   315→            @Override\n   316→            public void close() {}\n   317→        };\n   318→    }\n   319→\n   320→    @Override\n   321→    public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\n   322→        if (count() == 0)\n   323→            return Collections.emptyIterator();\n   324→\n   325→        if (!isCompressed())\n   326→            return uncompressedIterator();\n   327→\n   328→        // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\n   329→        // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\n   330→        // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\n   331→        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\n   332→            List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\n   333→            while (iterator.hasNext())\n   334→                records.add(iterator.next());\n   335→            return records.iterator();\n   336→        }\n   337→    }\n   338→\n   339→    @Override\n   340→    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n   341→        if (count() == 0) {\n   342→            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\n   343→        }\n   344→\n   345→        /*\n   346→         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\n   347→         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\n   348→         * as it will allocate new byte array.\n   349→         */\n   350→        if (!isCompressed())\n   351→            return uncompressedIterator();\n   352→\n   353→        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\n   354→        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\n   355→        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\n   356→    }\n   357→\n   358→    @Override\n   359→    public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n   360→        if (isCompressed())\n   361→            return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\n   362→        else\n   363→            return uncompressedIterator();\n   364→    }\n   365→\n   366→    @Override\n   367→    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\n   368→        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    @Override\n   372→    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\n   373→        long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n   374→        // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\n   375→        if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\n   376→            return;\n   377→\n   378→        byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\n   379→        buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n   380→        buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n   381→        long crc = computeChecksum();\n   382→        ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\n   383→    }\n   384→\n   385→    @Override\n   386→    public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\n   387→        buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n   388→    }\n   389→\n   390→    @Override\n   391→    public long checksum() {\n   392→        return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\n   393→    }\n   394→\n   395→    public boolean isValid() {\n   396→        return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\n   397→    }\n   398→\n   399→    private long computeChecksum() {\n   400→        return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n   401→    }\n   402→\n   403→    private byte attributes() {\n   404→        // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\n   405→        return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n   406→    }\n   407→\n   408→    @Override\n   409→    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n   410→        if (this == o)\n   411→            return true;\n   412→        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n   413→            return false;\n   414→\n   415→        DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\n   416→        return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\n   417→    }\n   418→\n   419→    @Override\n   420→    public int hashCode() {\n   421→        return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\n   422→    }\n   423→\n   424→    private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\n   425→                                          boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\n   426→        if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\n   427→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \" +\n   428→                    \"format v2 and above\");\n   429→\n   430→        byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\n   431→        if (isControl)\n   432→            attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\n   433→        if (type.id > 0)\n   434→            attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\n   435→        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n   436→            attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\n   437→        if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\n   438→            attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\n   439→        return attributes;\n   440→    }\n   441→\n   442→    public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   443→                                        byte magic,\n   444→                                        long producerId,\n   445→                                        short producerEpoch,\n   446→                                        int baseSequence,\n   447→                                        long baseOffset,\n   448→                                        long lastOffset,\n   449→                                        int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n   450→                                        TimestampType timestampType,\n   451→                                        long timestamp,\n   452→                                        boolean isTransactional,\n   453→                                        boolean isControlRecord) {\n   454→        int offsetDelta = (int) (lastOffset - baseOffset);\n   455→        writeHeader(buffer, baseOffset, offsetDelta, DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD, magic,\n   456→                    CompressionType.NONE, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, timestamp, producerId,\n   457→                    producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);\n   458→    }\n   459→\n   460→    public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   461→                                   long baseOffset,\n   462→                                   int lastOffsetDelta,\n   463→                                   int sizeInBytes,\n   464→                                   byte magic,\n   465→                                   CompressionType compressionType,\n   466→                                   TimestampType timestampType,\n   467→                                   long baseTimestamp,\n   468→                                   long maxTimestamp,\n   469→                                   long producerId,\n   470→                                   short epoch,\n   471→                                   int sequence,\n   472→                                   boolean isTransactional,\n   473→                                   boolean isControlBatch,\n   474→                                   boolean isDeleteHorizonSet,\n   475→                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n   476→                                   int numRecords) {\n   477→        if (magic < RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE)\n   478→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid magic value \" + magic);\n   479→        if (baseTimestamp < 0 && baseTimestamp != NO_TIMESTAMP)\n   480→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid message timestamp \" + baseTimestamp);\n   481→\n   482→        short attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType, timestampType, isTransactional, isControlBatch, isDeleteHorizonSet);\n   483→\n   484→        int position = buffer.position();\n   485→        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, baseOffset);\n   486→        buffer.putInt(position + LENGTH_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - LOG_OVERHEAD);\n   487→        buffer.putInt(position + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n   488→        buffer.put(position + MAGIC_OFFSET, magic);\n   489→        buffer.putShort(position + ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n   490→        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, baseTimestamp);\n   491→        buffer.putLong(position + MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n   492→        buffer.putInt(position + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET, lastOffsetDelta);\n   493→        buffer.putLong(position + PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET, producerId);\n   494→        buffer.putShort(position + PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n   495→        buffer.putInt(position + BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET, sequence);\n   496→        buffer.putInt(position + RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET, numRecords);\n   497→        long crc = Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n   498→        buffer.putInt(position + CRC_OFFSET, (int) crc);\n   499→        buffer.position(position + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n   500→    }\n   501→\n   502→    @Override\n   503→    public String toString() {\n   504→        return \"RecordBatch(magic=\" + magic() + \", offsets=[\" + baseOffset() + \", \" + lastOffset() + \"], \" +\n   505→                \"sequence=[\" + baseSequence() + \", \" + lastSequence() + \"], \" +\n   506→                \"partitionLeaderEpoch=\" + partitionLeaderEpoch() + \", \" +\n   507→                \"isTransactional=\" + isTransactional() + \", isControlBatch=\" + isControlBatch() + \", \" +\n   508→                \"compression=\" + compressionType() + \", timestampType=\" + timestampType() + \", crc=\" + checksum() + \")\";\n   509→    }\n   510→\n   511→    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {\n   512→        Iterator<Record> iterator = records.iterator();\n   513→        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n   514→            return 0;\n   515→\n   516→        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n   517→        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n   518→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   519→            Record record = iterator.next();\n   520→            int offsetDelta = (int) (record.offset() - baseOffset);\n   521→            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n   522→                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n   523→ record.headers());\n   526→        }\n   527→        return size;\n   528→    }\n   529→\n   530→    public static int sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord> records) {\n   531→        Iterator<SimpleRecord> iterator = records.iterator();\n   532→        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n   533→            return 0;\n   534→\n   535→        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n   536→        int offsetDelta = 0;\n   537→        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n   538→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   539→            SimpleRecord record = iterator.next();\n   540→            if 548→\n   549→    /**\n   550→     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. }\n   557→\n   558→    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {\n   559→        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)\n   560→            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;\n   561→        return sequence + increment;\n   562→    }\n   563→\n   564→    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {\n   565→        if (sequence < decrement)\n   566→            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;\n   567→        return sequence - decrement;\n   568→    }\n   569→\n   570→    // visible for testing\n   571→    abstract class RecordIterator implements CloseableIterator<Record> {\n   572→        private final Long logAppendTime;\n   573→        private final long baseOffset;\n   574→        private final long baseTimestamp;\n   575→        private final int baseSequence;\n   576→        private final int numRecords;\n   577→        private int readRecords = 0;\n   578→\n   579→        RecordIterator() {\n   580→            this.logAppendTime = timestampType() == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ? maxTimestamp() : null;\n   581→            this.baseOffset = baseOffset();\n   582→            this.baseTimestamp = baseTimestamp();\n   583→            this.baseSequence = baseSequence();\n   584→            int numRecords = count();\n   585→            if (numRecords < 0)\n   586→    @Override\n   597→        public Record next() {\n   598→            if (readRecords >= numRecords)\n   599→                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n   600→\n   601→ Validate that the actual size of the batch is equal to declared size\n   605→                // by checking that after reading declared number of items, there no items left\n   606→                // (overflow case, i.e. reading past buffer end is checked elsewhere).\n   607→                if (!ensureNoneRemaining())\n   608→                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, records still remaining in file\");\n   609→            }\n   610→            return rec;\n   611→        }\n   612→\n   613→        protected abstract Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime);\n   614→\n   615→        protected abstract boolean ensureNoneRemaining();\n   616→\n   617→        @Override\n   618→        public void remove() {\n   619→            throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n   620→        }\n   621→\n   622→    }\n   623→\n   624→    // visible for testing\n   625→    abstract class StreamRecordIterator extends RecordIterator {\n   626→        private final InputStream inputStream;\n   627→\n   628→        StreamRecordIterator(InputStream inputStream) {\n   629→ logAppendTime) throws IOException;\n   634→\n   635→        @Override\n   636→        protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n   637→            try {\n   638→                return doReadRecord(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n   639→            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n   640→ batch\", e);\n   652→            }\n   653→        }\n   654→\n   655→        @Override\n   656→        public void close() {\n   657→            try {\n   658→                inputStream.close();\n     public boolean isControlBatch() {\n   727→            return loadBatchHeader().isControlBatch();\n   728→        }\n   729→\n   730→        @Override\n   731→        public int See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.NoSuchElementException;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n\n/**\n * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:\n *\n * RecordBatch =>\n *  BaseOffset => Int64\n *  Length => Int32\n *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n *  Magic => Int8\n *  CRC => Uint32\n *  Attributes => Int16\n *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n *  ProducerId => Int64\n *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n *  BaseSequence => Int32\n *  RecordsCount => Int32\n *  Records => [Record]\n *\n * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized\n * directly following the count of the number of records.\n *\n * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is\n * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret\n * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC\n * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by\n * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.\n *\n * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence\n * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the\n * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following\n * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence\n * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an\n * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence error, which is typically fatal. The base sequence number must be preserved for\n * duplicate checking: the broker checks incoming Produce requests for duplicates by verifying that the first and\n * last sequence numbers of the incoming batch match the last from that producer.\n *\n * Note that if all of the records in a batch are removed during compaction, the broker may still retain an empty\n * batch header in order to preserve the producer sequence information as described above. These empty batches\n * are retained only until either a new sequence number is written by the corresponding producer or the producerId\n * is expired from lack of activity.\n *\n * There is no similar need to preserve the timestamp from the original batch after compaction. The BaseTimestamp\n * field therefore reflects the timestamp of the first record in the batch in most cases. If the batch is empty, the\n * BaseTimestamp will be set to -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP). If the delete horizon flag is set to 1, the BaseTimestamp\n * will be set to the time at which tombstone records and aborted transaction markers in the batch should be removed.\n *\n * Similarly, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the maximum timestamp of the current records if the timestamp type\n * is CREATE_TIME. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, on the other hand, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the timestamp set\n * by the broker and is preserved after compaction. Additionally, the MaxTimestamp of an empty batch always retains\n * the previous value prior to becoming empty.\n *\n * The current attributes are given below:\n *\n *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n *  | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |\n *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n\n    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte magic() {\n        return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void ensureValid() {\n        if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n                    \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n\n        if (!isValid())\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n                    + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.\n     *\n     * @return The base timestamp\n     */\n    public long baseTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long maxTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public TimestampType timestampType() {\n        return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long baseOffset() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long lastOffset() {\n        return baseOffset() + lastOffsetDelta();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long producerId() {\n        return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public short producerEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getShort(PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int baseSequence() {\n        return buffer.getInt(BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int lastOffsetDelta() {\n        return buffer.getInt(LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int lastSequence() {\n        int baseSequence = baseSequence();\n        if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\n            return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\n        return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CompressionType compressionType() {\n        return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return LOG_OVERHEAD + buffer.getInt(LENGTH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int count() {\n        return buffer.getInt(RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer countOrNull() {\n        return count();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\n        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isTransactional() {\n        return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\n        return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n        if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\n            return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\n        else\n            return OptionalLong.empty();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isControlBatch() {\n        return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\n        final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\n\n        if (skipKeyValue) {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        } else {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return new RecordIterator() {\n            @Override\n            protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n                try {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\");\n                }\n            }\n            @Override\n            protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n                return !buffer.hasRemaining();\n            }\n            @Override\n            public void close() {}\n        };\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\n        if (count() == 0)\n            return Collections.emptyIterator();\n\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\n        // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\n        // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\n            List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\n            while (iterator.hasNext())\n                records.add(iterator.next());\n            return records.iterator();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (count() == 0) {\n            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\n        }\n\n        /*\n         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\n         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\n         * as it will allocate new byte array.\n         */\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\n        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\n        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (isCompressed())\n            return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\n        else\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\n        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\n        long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\n        if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\n            return;\n\n        byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\n        buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        long crc = computeChecksum();\n        ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\n        buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long checksum() {\n        return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    public boolean isValid() {\n        return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\n    }\n\n    private long computeChecksum() {\n        return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private byte attributes() {\n        // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\n        return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\n        return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\n    }\n\n    private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\n                                          boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \" +\n                    \"format v2 and above\");\n\n        byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\n        if (isControl)\n            attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\n        if (type.id > 0)\n            attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\n        if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\n            attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\n        return attributes;\n    }\n\n    public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                        byte magic,\n                                        long producerId,\n                                        short producerEpoch,\n                                        int baseSequence,\n                                        long baseOffset,\n                                        long lastOffset,\n                                        int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                        TimestampType timestampType,\n                                        long timestamp,\n                                        boolean isTransactional,\n                                        boolean isControlRecord) {\n        int offsetDelta = (int) (lastOffset - baseOffset);\n        writeHeader(buffer, baseOffset, offsetDelta, DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD, magic,\n                    CompressionType.NONE, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, timestamp, producerId,\n                    producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);\n    }\n\n    public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                   long baseOffset,\n                                   int lastOffsetDelta,\n                                   int sizeInBytes,\n                                   byte magic,\n                                   CompressionType compressionType,\n                                   TimestampType timestampType,\n                                   long baseTimestamp,\n                                   long maxTimestamp,\n                                   long producerId,\n                                   short epoch,\n                                   int sequence,\n                                   boolean isTransactional,\n                                   boolean isControlBatch,\n                                   boolean isDeleteHorizonSet,\n                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                   int numRecords) {\n        if (magic < RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid magic value \" + magic);\n        if (baseTimestamp < 0 && baseTimestamp != NO_TIMESTAMP)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid message timestamp \" + baseTimestamp);\n\n        short attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType, timestampType, isTransactional, isControlBatch, isDeleteHorizonSet);\n\n        int position = buffer.position();\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, baseOffset);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LENGTH_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - LOG_OVERHEAD);\n        buffer.putInt(position + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n        buffer.put(position + MAGIC_OFFSET, magic);\n        buffer.putShort(position + ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, baseTimestamp);\n        buffer.putLong(position + MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET, lastOffsetDelta);\n        buffer.putLong(position + PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET, producerId);\n        buffer.putShort(position + PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n        buffer.putInt(position + BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET, sequence);\n        buffer.putInt(position + RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET, numRecords);\n        long crc = Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n        buffer.putInt(position + CRC_OFFSET, (int) crc);\n        buffer.position(position + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"RecordBatch(magic=\" + magic() + \", offsets=[\" + baseOffset() + \", \" + lastOffset() + \"], \" +\n                \"sequence=[\" + baseSequence() + \", \" + lastSequence() + \"], \" +\n                \"partitionLeaderEpoch=\" + partitionLeaderEpoch() + \", \" +\n                \"isTransactional=\" + isTransactional() + \", isControlBatch=\" + isControlBatch() + \", \" +\n                \"compression=\" + compressionType() + \", timestampType=\" + timestampType() + \", crc=\" + checksum() + \")\";\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {\n        Iterator<Record> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            Record record = iterator.next();\n            int offsetDelta = (int) (record.offset() - baseOffset);\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord> records) {\n        Iterator<SimpleRecord> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        int offsetDelta = 0;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            SimpleRecord record = iterator.next();\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta++, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This\n     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression\n     * algorithm used.\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }\n\n    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {\n        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)\n            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;\n        return sequence + increment;\n    }\n\n    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {\n        if (sequence < decrement)\n            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;\n        return sequence - decrement;\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class RecordIterator implements CloseableIterator<Record> {\n        private final Long logAppendTime;\n        private final long baseOffset;\n        private final long baseTimestamp;\n        private final int baseSequence;\n        private final int numRecords;\n        private int readRecords = 0;\n\n        RecordIterator() {\n            this.logAppendTime = timestampType() == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ? maxTimestamp() : null;\n            this.baseOffset = baseOffset();\n            this.baseTimestamp = baseTimestamp();\n            this.baseSequence = baseSequence();\n            int numRecords = count();\n            if (numRecords < 0)\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Found invalid record count \" + numRecords + \" in magic v\" +\n                        magic() + \" batch\");\n            this.numRecords = numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean hasNext() {\n            return readRecords < numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Record next() {\n            if (readRecords >= numRecords)\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n\n            readRecords++;\n            Record rec = readNext(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            if (readRecords == numRecords) {\n                // Validate that the actual size of the batch is equal to declared size\n                // by checking that after reading declared number of items, there no items left\n                // (overflow case, i.e. reading past buffer end is checked elsewhere).\n                if (!ensureNoneRemaining())\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, records still remaining in file\");\n            }\n            return rec;\n        }\n\n        protected abstract Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime);\n\n        protected abstract boolean ensureNoneRemaining();\n\n        @Override\n        public void remove() {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n        }\n\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class StreamRecordIterator extends RecordIterator {\n        private final InputStream inputStream;\n\n        StreamRecordIterator(InputStream inputStream) {\n            super();\n            this.inputStream = inputStream;\n        }\n\n        abstract Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException;\n\n        @Override\n        protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n            try {\n                return doReadRecord(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\", e);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to decompress record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n            try {\n                return inputStream.read() == -1;\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Error checking for remaining bytes after reading batch\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            try {\n                inputStream.close();\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to close record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    static class DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch extends FileLogInputStream.FileChannelRecordBatch {\n\n        DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch(long offset,\n                                      byte magic,\n                                      FileRecords fileRecords,\n                                      int position,\n                                      int batchSize) {\n            super(offset, magic, fileRecords, position, batchSize);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected RecordBatch toMemoryRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n            return new DefaultRecordBatch(buffer);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long baseOffset() {\n            return offset;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long lastOffset() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().lastOffset();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long producerId() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().producerId();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public }\n\n}\n","numLines":742,"startLine":1,"totalLines":742}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01TU3vRPEo9pXaiJ8KG1JdsE","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01AgL1eTFW2fC1LPxTx2e8hb","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java","old_string":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","new_string":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka protocol. In old versions of the record format\n * (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are the fundamental unit of serialization for Kafka message logs.\n * They encapsulate multiple records along with metadata such as offsets, timestamps, compression information, and\n * producer information (for idempotence and transactions). The batch structure allows for efficient storage and\n * transmission of multiple records with a single header overhead.\n *\n * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> Implementations may or may not be thread-safe. Callers should consult the implementation\n * documentation for specific thread-safety guarantees. Generally, record batches are immutable after construction\n * (except for {@link MutableRecordBatch}), but this is not universally guaranteed.\n *\n * <p><b>Magic Versions:</b> The magic version indicates the record format:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Magic 0: Legacy format with no timestamp or producer information</li>\n *   <li>Magic 1: Added timestamp support</li>\n *   <li>Magic 2+: Added producer ID, sequence numbers, transactional markers, and control records</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see {@link MutableRecordBatch} for mutable variants\n * @see {@link MemoryRecords} for in-memory batch collections\n * @see {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for the magic v2+ concrete implementation\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":13685,"cache_read_input_tokens":36542,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":13685},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d30914fa-34e4-412f-bab7-b4baee20feb3","uuid":"cf6ba727-805f-4096-94d0-d2e30ba38391"}

In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","newString":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka protocol. In old versions of the record format\n * (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are the fundamental unit of serialization for Kafka message logs.\n * They encapsulate multiple records along with metadata such as offsets, timestamps, compression information, and\n * producer information (for idempotence and transactions). The batch structure allows for efficient storage and\n * transmission of multiple records with a single header overhead.\n *\n * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> Implementations may or may not be thread-safe. Callers should consult the implementation\n * documentation for specific thread-safety guarantees. Generally, record batches are immutable after construction\n * (except for {@link MutableRecordBatch}), but this is not universally guaranteed.\n *\n * <p><b>Magic Versions:</b> The magic version indicates the record format:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Magic 0: Legacy format with no timestamp or producer information</li>\n *   <li>Magic 1: Added timestamp support</li>\n *   <li>Magic 2+: Added producer ID, sequence numbers, transactional markers, and control records</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see {@link MutableRecordBatch} for mutable variants\n * @see {@link MemoryRecords} for in-memory batch collections\n * @see {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for the magic v2+ concrete implementation\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","originalFile":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":26,"oldLines":10,"newStart":26,"newLines":30,"lines":[" import java.util.OptionalLong;"," "," /**","- * A record batch is a container for records.
